skill.name: video-edit
skill.description: Routes video-edit requests to RunComfy models for restyling, background changes, packaging swaps, outfit edits, and motion transfer. It provides model selection guidance, prompt patterns, and RunComfy CLI invocation examples.
skill.summary: Route video edits to the right RunComfy model and produce identity-stable transformations.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found no malicious intent or prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md. Most crypto, path traversal, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from markdown text, placeholder URLs, or model names. The remaining risk is medium because the skill instructs use of a local CLI that sends prompts and media URLs to RunComfy and writes output files.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill repeatedly instructs agents to install and run the RunComfy CLI. This is expected for the skill, but it expands execution risk because commands run locally and depend on a third-party package and authenticated CLI state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Commands are explicit in markdown examples and align with the declared RunComfy workflow. Confidence is high, but no dynamic shell construction or prompt-to-shell interpolation was found.

medium_findings.title: Remote Media Processing and Download Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill sends prompts, source media URLs, and reference media URLs to RunComfy services, then downloads generated results. This is legitimate for cloud video editing but creates privacy and data handling risk for sensitive media.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is described directly, including POST requests, polling, and output downloads. It appears intentional and scoped to RunComfy endpoints, not covert exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Output File Handling
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill documents API token storage in a hidden home-directory config file and instructs output downloads to a local output directory. This is normal CLI behavior, but users should understand where credentials and generated files are stored.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and output behavior are clearly stated. The risk is moderate because no instruction reads or transmits the token outside the RunComfy CLI workflow.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alerts Are Placeholder URL False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static traversal findings point to example media URLs using ellipses such as https://... and not to local relative paths or filesystem traversal logic. No evidence found that the skill reads arbitrary local paths through these examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visibly placeholder URL syntax inside example JSON. There is no path join, file read, or traversal target in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static blocker findings for weak cryptography and reconnaissance are caused by natural-language model names, markdown prose, or terms like aspect ratio. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, hashing, scanning, or system reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only markdown descriptions and schema tables at the cited locations. No executable cryptographic or reconnaissance behavior appears in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes install, login, and run commands for a local CLI. These commands are central to the skill, but marketplace users should review the package source and command arguments before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present and intended for execution. They use fixed model identifiers and JSON examples, reducing but not eliminating operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party API Transfer of User Media
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow submits video, image, prompt, and output requests to RunComfy infrastructure. This is not malicious, but it is sensitive for private, regulated, or unreleased media.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly explains that the CLI posts to the Model API and downloads generated output. The behavior is declared and limited to the service workflow.

content.value_statement: Video edits often fail when the wrong model is selected for the job. This skill routes each request to a suitable RunComfy model and supplies focused prompting patterns.
actual_capabilities: Selects Wan 2.7 Edit-Video for general restyle, background swap, packaging swap, and commercial polish edits.
Selects Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control when a reference video should drive target character motion.
Selects Lucy Edit Restyle for lightweight outfit, lighting, and localized identity-stable changes.
Provides model-specific input fields, duration limits, resolution options, and audio preservation settings.
Shows RunComfy CLI commands for submitting video edits and choosing local output directories.
Gives prompt patterns that emphasize identity, motion, framing, and audio preservation.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and valid authentication before model calls can run.
Source videos and references must be reachable URLs accepted by the selected RunComfy model.
Each edit uses one route per call, so complex multi-step transformations require separate runs.
Model limits apply, including duration, resolution, orientation, and aspect ratio constraints.

use_cases.title: Refresh Product Clips
use_cases.description: Swap packaging designs or product backgrounds while preserving camera motion and framing.

use_cases.title: Adapt Creator Videos
use_cases.description: Restyle short talking-head clips while preserving face, pose, lip movement, and original audio.

use_cases.title: Transfer Reference Motion
use_cases.description: Map motion from a reference clip onto a target character using the Kling motion route.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Background Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one visual change
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this video with RunComfy. Preserve the subject, pose, lip movement, and audio. Change only the background to a clean studio setting.

prompt_templates.title: Product Packaging Swap
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate edit using a reference image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the video URL and reference image URL I provide. Replace the packaging design with the reference design, while preserving hand motion, framing, and lighting.

prompt_templates.title: Identity-Stable Outfit Restyle
prompt_templates.prompt: Route this as a lightweight restyle. Change the subject outfit to professional business attire, preserve face and motion, and keep the original aspect ratio.

prompt_templates.title: Motion-Control Transfer
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced route selection with reference motion
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the motion reference video to drive the target character. Preserve the target character identity, keep the main motion accurate, and keep original sound if supported.

output_examples.input: A user asks to replace the background in a short talking-head video and keep the speaker audio.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Wan 2.7 Edit-Video, prioritizes face and lip movement preservation, and includes the origin audio setting.

output_examples.input: A user provides a target character image and a dance reference clip.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control and frames the request as reference motion transfer.

output_examples.input: A user wants a walking portrait clip changed to business attire.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Lucy Edit Restyle and keeps the prompt focused on outfit, face, and motion preservation.

best_practices: Use one clear edit direction per run, such as background, outfit, packaging, lighting, or motion transfer.
Put preservation goals first in the prompt before describing the requested visual change.
Use trusted media URLs and avoid submitting private or regulated content without approval.
anti_patterns: Do not combine unrelated edits in one request when separate model runs would be clearer.
Do not use reference images unless the edit needs direct appearance or design transfer.
Do not submit secrets, private media, or unreleased assets to remote processing without review.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It routes video edit requests to RunComfy models and gives model-specific prompting and CLI guidance.

faq.question: Does it edit videos locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the local RunComfy CLI to submit work to RunComfy model services.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it preserve a speaker voice or audio?
faq.answer: Some routes include audio preservation settings, but final support depends on the selected model.

faq.question: What inputs are usually required?
faq.answer: Most routes need a prompt and a source video URL. Some routes also need an image or reference video.

faq.question: What security review should users perform?
faq.answer: Review the RunComfy CLI package, confirm endpoint trust, and avoid submitting sensitive media without approval.
